extraction.one_line_summary: LAFAY Victor won from the break, catching and dropping Carboni and Campenaerts on the final 3km climb after their premature attacks depleted them.
tactical_patterns: Campenaerts' repeated attacks before final climb (multiple efforts cited) backfired catastrophically when gradients increased, dropped by both rivals.
Carboni attacked only Campenaerts rather than waiting for final climb, allowing fresh Lafay to bridge and counter decisively.
Over 60km of attacking before break established suggests stronger-than-expected wind impact on peloton control despite non-mountainous terrain.
Gaviria prioritized intermediate sprint points despite being in winning break, revealing jersey competition trumping stage hunting for UAE.

rider_signals.LAFAY Victor: positive: Timed effort perfectly by sitting on during premature attacks, then dominated 3km climb while rivals paid for earlier moves
rider_signals.CAMPENAERTS Victor: negative: Wasteful tactical approach with multiple failed attacks before final climb, completely empty when gradient hit
rider_signals.CARBONI Giovanni: negative: Attacked too early with Campenaerts instead of conserving for steeper finale, caught and dropped by fresher Lafay
rider_signals.EWAN Caleb: negative: Abandoned with knee pain after 60km of chaotic attacking, suggesting sprint leadouts may have structural injury vulnerability
rider_signals.GAVIRIA Fernando: negative: Crashed after attacking on main climb then chased back, indicating desperation after prioritizing points over stage positioning
